source: Distilled from the Graham-Klarman value tradition. All card language below is paraphrase unless a primary-source quotation is added and verified.
_note: Runtime lens. It seeks verified discount and permanent-loss protection rather than price strength.

master_intro.one_line: Protect capital by demanding a meaningful discount to a defensible estimate of intrinsic value.
pillars: Hold cash when opportunity is weak
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens asks whether downside support and valuation evidence existed before capital was committed.

strength_intro: One thing you did well through this lens:

position_sizing.rule: Size from estimated permanent-loss downside and uncertainty, not only upside potential.
position_sizing.quote: Capital protection comes before return maximization. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. What permanent-loss scenario was used to cap the position?

averaging_down.rubric_unit: Add only when the verified discount widens
averaging_down.rule: Add only after re-estimating intrinsic value and confirming that the margin of safety widened for reasons unrelated to thesis deterioration.
averaging_down.quote: A lower price helps only if value and downside support remain intact. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. What new valuation work showed a wider margin of safety rather than a damaged asset?

exit_discipline.rubric_unit: Exit when value is realized or the estimate breaks
exit_discipline.rule: Exit when price reaches defensible value, intrinsic value falls, or the original margin-of-safety calculation is invalidated.
exit_discipline.quote: The sale decision follows value and downside, not the emotional need to lock in a result. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: Of the profitable exits, {winner_early}% later rose. Which exits reflected value realization, and which lacked an updated value estimate?

diversification.rubric_unit: Use moderate diversification against estimation error
diversification.rule: Diversify enough to survive valuation error while avoiding positions with no adequate margin of safety.
diversification.quote: Diversification can protect against analytical error, but it cannot make an overpriced asset safe. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. How many independent valuation errors could damage the portfolio at once?

holding_period.rubric_unit: Hold until value is realized or invalidated
holding_period.rule: Set the horizon from the value-realization path and catalyst; do not turn missing realization into an indefinite hold.
holding_period.quote: Patience needs a valuation thesis and a path for value to matter. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, did the value-realization path change, or did the horizon change only because the price stayed below cost?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Separate value realization from market beta
alpha_beta.rule: Decompose market and factor exposure before attributing return to the margin-of-safety analysis.
alpha_beta.quote: A rising market can obscure whether the valuation thesis added value. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. What return came from closing the value gap rather than broad factor exposure?

entry_style.rubric_unit: Prefer verified discount over price strength
entry_style.rule: Enter only after estimating intrinsic value, downside, and a meaningful margin of safety; weakness alone is not enough.
entry_style.quote: Price is attractive only in relation to defensible value. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, what valuation gap and downside support justified entry rather than simply buying weakness?
